mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above a recessionary threshold of 6%, it signals a deep and persistent contraction in the labor market, leading to reduced aggregate demand, lower corporate earnings, and negative equity returns over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:NBER recession dating criteria often include sharp rises in unemployment.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE is above 6.0% and rising.

mechanism_narrative: Aggressive and sustained cuts to the Federal Funds Rate signal strong central bank support for the economy and financial markets, lowering the discount rate for future earnings and driving positive equity returns as liquidity improves.
evidence_refs: fact:The 'Fed model' links equity earnings yields to bond yields, which are influenced by the Fed Funds rate.
failure_condition: The correlation between the 3-month change in FEDFUNDS and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is not significantly negative (i.e., rank IC > -0.03) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in new residential housing starts (HOUST) is a leading indicator of a broad-based slowdown in consumer spending and investment, negatively impacting sectors from construction to finance and predicting lower aggregate corporate profits.
evidence_refs: fact:The 2007-2008 housing market collapse preceded the broader financial crisis.
failure_condition: The 12-month change in HOUST has a rolling 3-year rank correlation with 60-day forward S&P 500 returns that is not statistically significant from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial production, defined as a 6-month percentage change falling below -5%, indicates a severe recessionary shock to the manufacturing and supply side of the economy, leading to immediate negative revisions in earnings forecasts and stock prices.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production is a key component of the Index of Coincident Economic Indicators.
failure_condition: In the 20 trading days following a trigger event, the S&P 500 posts positive returns more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment is high (UNRATE > 6%) and the Fed Funds rate is low (FEDFUNDS < 1.5%), monetary policy becomes ineffective (a 'liquidity trap'). This combination signals a severe credit crunch and demand collapse that monetary easing cannot fix, leading to negative equity returns.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 shows statistically significant positive returns over a 60-day horizon when both trigger conditions are met.

mechanism_narrative: During a recession, negative shocks to retail sales have an amplified negative effect on equity returns. A sharp month-over-month decline in RSAFS confirms consumer retrenchment, leading to downward earnings revisions and stock price declines.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending constitutes roughly two-thirds of US GDP.
failure_condition: The negative return premium following a trigger event during NBER-dated recessions is not statistically different from the premium during expansions.

mechanism_narrative: The monthly change in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) is a timely and direct measure of economic momentum. A negative print signals economic contraction and prompts an immediate negative reaction in equity markets over a short horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:The PAYEMS release is a major market-moving event each month.
failure_condition: The average 5-day S&P 500 return following a negative PAYEMS print (month-over-month change < 0) is not statistically different from zero.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of falling inflation (declining CPIAUCSL year-over-year) and a low Federal Funds rate (FEDFUNDS < 2%) signals a deflationary bust. This environment of falling prices and weak demand is highly detrimental to corporate pricing power and profits, leading to negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 12-month pct_change is negative AND FEDFUNDS < 2.0
evidence_refs: fact:The Great Depression and Japan's 'Lost Decade' were characterized by deflation.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 exhibits positive average 60-day returns during periods when both trigger conditions are met.
